2606.13919.title: GMN4AD: Graph Matching Network for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis with Test-Time Domain Adaptation using Multi-centered Structure Magnetic Resonance Imaging
2606.13919.paper_summary: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions of older adults, with prevalence expected to rise significantly in the coming years. Early diagnosis, particularly during the mild cognitive impairment (MCI) stage, is critical for timely intervention. Structural Magnetic Resonance Imaging (sMRI) has emerged as a key modality for detecting AD-related brain changes, but traditional graph-based approaches often struggle with modality and inter-site heterogeneity, limiting diagnostic performance. In this paper, we propose Graph Matching Network for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis (GMN4AD), designed to model interactions between heterogeneous brain graphs derived from neuroimaging data. Unlike conventional methods that treat each brain graph independently, GMN4AD leverages graph matching to capture cross-graph relationships, enhancing diagnostic precision. Furthermore, we introduce a test-time domain adaptation strategy that combines contrastive learning to mitigate domain shifts during inference. Extensive experiments on three public AD datasets demonstrate that GMN4AD achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods, offering a robust and generalizable solution for AD diagnosis.
2606.13919.paper_summary_zh: 阿茲海默症（AD）是一種進行性神經退行性疾病，影響數百萬老年人，預計在未來幾年內其患病率將顯著上升。<lb/>早期診斷，特別是在輕度認知障礙（MCI）階段，對於及時干預至關重要。<lb/>結構性磁共振成像（sMRI）已成為檢測與AD相關的大腦變化的關鍵方法，但傳統的基於圖形的方法通常在模態和跨站點異質性方面面臨挑戰，限制了診斷性能。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了阿茲海默症診斷的圖匹配網絡（GMN4AD），旨在建模來自神經影像數據的異質大腦圖之間的互動。<lb/>與將每個大腦圖獨立處理的傳統方法不同，GMN4AD利用圖匹配來捕捉跨圖關係，提高診斷精度。<lb/>此外，我們引入了一種測試時域適應策略，結合對比學習以減輕推理過程中的域偏移。<lb/>在三個公共AD數據集上進行的廣泛實驗表明，GMN4AD的性能優於最先進的方法，提供了一種穩健且可泛化的AD診斷解決方案。
2606.13919.author: Chen Zhao et.al.
2606.13919.authors: Chen Zhao, Huan Huang, Yixin Xie, Jiajing Huang, Weihua Zhou, Nandakumar Narayanan
